preview_text:
The statistical model favors Australia narrowly, assigning it a 35% win probability against USA at 34%, making this one of the most evenly matched pairings in the forecast. The model's assessment reflects considerable uncertainty: the gap between the two favorites is negligible, and both remain well below 40%.

The draw looms large in the model's view at 32%, suggesting a competitive encounter where neither side is expected to establish decisive control. The W/D/L spread is remarkably flat across all three outcomes, indicating the match could plausibly resolve in any direction. Tactical balance and marginal differences in underlying strength appear to drive a genuinely competitive scenario rather than a tilted contest.

Sportsbooks and Polymarket both diverge sharply from the model, crediting USA with roughly 54% win probability against Australia's 21%—a gap of roughly 33 percentage points favoring the USA. Both betting venues also compress the draw to around 24–25%, well below the model's midpoint. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the divergence is large enough that users should note the disagreement and consider what information each source may be weighing differently.
